LanguageData.JanitorsCloset.CleaningTool.WrongFilthCa: Tool not suited to this filth
LanguageData.JanitorsCloset.CleaningTool.SpecialtyExp: The cleaning speed bonus from this tool applies to dry filth. Wet filth is cleaned at regular speed.\n\nA pawn carrying this tool will also gravitate toward dry filth when choosing what to clean next, and finish the dry filth in a cluster before any wet filth in the same trip.

The cleaning speed bonus from this tool applies to wet filth. Dry filth is cleaned at regular speed.\n\nA pawn carrying this tool will also gravitate toward wet filth when choosing what to clean next, and finish the wet filth in a cluster before any dry filth in the same trip.
The cleaning speed bonus from this tool applies while clearing polluted terrain. Ordinary filth is cleaned at regular speed.
The cleaning speed bonus from this tool also applies while clearing accumulated snow or windblown sand from the ground.

This tool dematerialises every layer of filth on the target tile and its eight neighbours in a single pass, not just the topmost piece on the target tile. The per-tile cleaning speed is unchanged; the trick is that nine tiles are cleared at once.
LanguageData.JanitorsCloset.CleaningAssignment.Desc.A: This tool's bearer will pick up cleaning jobs regardless of whether the filth is indoors or outdoors. Click to restrict to indoors only.
LanguageData.JanitorsCloset.CleaningAssignment.Desc.I: This tool's bearer will ignore filth outdoors, only cleaning rooms enclosed by walls and roof. Click to restrict to outdoors only.
LanguageData.JanitorsCloset.CleaningAssignment.Desc.O: This tool's bearer will ignore indoor filth, only cleaning outside or in unenclosed spaces. Click to clear the restriction.
LanguageData.JanitorsCloset.CleaningArea.Desc.None: This tool's bearer will pick up cleaning jobs anywhere on the map. Click to restrict cleaning to a player-painted area.
LanguageData.JanitorsCloset.CleaningArea.Desc.Restric: This tool's bearer will only clean filth inside the "{0}" area. If that area is renamed or deleted, the restriction is dropped. Click to choose a different area or clear the restriction.
LanguageData.JanitorsCloset.Thought.FreshlyCleanedRoo: That room was spotless. I'm glad we have a janitor around.
LanguageData.JanitorsCloset.Settings.Diagnostics.Enab: Master switch for diagnostic logging. When off, no diagnostics are written regardless of the individual channel toggles below.
LanguageData.JanitorsCloset.Settings.Diagnostics.Head: Diagnostic channels
LanguageData.JanitorsCloset.Settings.Diagnostics.Subt: Each channel logs at most once per state change. Verbose — only enable while investigating an issue.
LanguageData.JanitorsCloset.Settings.Diagnostics.AI.T: Logs the per-pawn cleaning-category preference and any in-trip filth queue reorders or drops. One line per pawn per cleaning scan.
LanguageData.JanitorsCloset.Settings.Diagnostics.Anim: Logs whether the broom/mop/sprayer sweep anim is applied each frame the pawn redraws, and the reason if rejected. One line per pawn per state change.
LanguageData.JanitorsCloset.Settings.Diagnostics.Rend: Logs every state change in vanilla's weapon render path for tool-bearing pawns. Useful when an anim never starts — tells you whether vanilla is even calling the render hook. Spammy; leave off unless chasing a missing-anim bug.
LanguageData.JanitorsCloset.Settings.Diagnostics.Stat: Logs cleaning-speed, weather-buildup and pollution-clearing bonuses as they apply or get suppressed each tick of a cleaning toil.
LanguageData.JanitorsCloset.Settings.Diagnostics.Soun: Logs every cleaning sustainer the mod swaps in (dirt, fluid, glittervacuum, etc.) plus any tool that has nothing to swap.
LanguageData.JanitorsCloset.Settings.Diagnostics.Trac: Logs when tracked-in filth gets attributed to a freshly-cleaned room (the "they just dirtied my floor" thought trigger).
